texto: Zazen is the dragons roar. This dragons roar is the wind passing and whistling through a hollow tree. This can be a metaphor for your Zazen practice and or even your life. If you can let go, greatly let go, this dragons roar can manifest through you. It is much bigger than the individual.
explicacao: Citação de Shoryu Bradley sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Zazen is the dragons roar. This dragons roar is the wind passing and whistling through a hollow t…” articula reflexao, buddhism, meditation em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como meditation e zen.

frase_original: Zazen is the dragons roar. This dragons roar is the wind passing and whistling through a hollow tree. This can be a metaphor for your Zazen practice and or even your life. If you can let go, greatly let go, this dragons roar can manifest through you. It is much bigger than the individual.

texto: The study of the self. This is the foundational practice of Buddhism. Basically it's the whole point. So what is the point... What is this really about? To study the self addresses everything we encounter in life. We can't encounter anything but the self actually. It's about our entire experience of living. Of life and death. It addresses questions such as Who am I?, What is the nature of my suffering? ... And my well-being, What is my place in this world?, What is my relationship to the things I encounter? ... And ultimately How do I live a life of meaning and energy?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Shoryu Bradley que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “The study of the self. This is the foundational practice of Buddhism. Basically it's the whole po…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, buddhism, zen, com foco em luto e estudos.

texto: Aggression, Violence, Exploitation, Depression, Despair, Prejudice, War, Intolerance, Poverty, Are all a result of a misunderstanding of the nature of Self.
